I
t
Re:
Fern Polrrrcal PnacrrcEs CoulrrssroN
428 J Street r Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
February 8,2005
Stephen P. Pfanler, City Attorney
Bannan, Green, Frank &Terzian,LLP
555 South Flower Street, Suite 2700
Los Angeles, CA 90071
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-04-250
Dear Mr. Pfanler:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding South Pasadena City
Councilmember David Margrave on the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform
Act (the "Act').1 The Act requires the Commission to provide written advice to any person
whose duties under the Act are in question or to that person's authorized representative. (Section
831 1a@); regulation 18329(a) - copy enclosed.) However, the Commission may also decline to
provide written advice if it would be inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest to do
so. (Regulation I 8329(bX8XF).)
Formal written advice is the application of law to a given set of facts provided by the
requestor and has the purpose of conferring immunity to the requestor. (Section 831 14;
regulation 18329b)O).) After a careful review of the facts you submitted, we decline to provide
written advice to you at this time. The same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a
pending investigation by the Enforcement Division. Therefore, it would be inappropriate for the
Commission to provide advice on this matter while the enforcanent investigation is still pending.
Our decision is not a determination of the questions posed in your request. ln addition,
nothing in the Act prevents an official from seeking legal advice from you orprivate counsel,
duringour investigation. If the Enforcement Division resolves'the matter with a finding that no
violation has occurred, you may resubmit your request for advice at that time.
l. GovemmentCodesections8l000-gl0l4.Commissionregulationsappearattitle2,sectionslsl0g*
18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

Therefore, it would be inappropriate for the Commission to provide advice on this matter while the enforcement investigation is still pending.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether South Pasadena City Councilmember David Margrave complies with the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide written advice because the same or similar issues are the subject of a pending Enforcement Division investigation, making it inappropriate to advise at this time. The requestor may resubmit the request after the investigation is resolved.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections with explicit headers are present, but the substance of the request and the declination reasoning are clearly stated in the body text. File number W-04-250 indicates this is a written advice request file.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether South Pasadena City Councilmember David Margrave complies with the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: After a careful review of the facts you submitted, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. The same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division. Therefore, it would be inappropriate for the Commission to provide advice on this matter while the enforcement investigation is still pending.
